blockingIssues.description: Annotated/ascribed direct `Result` let-bindings still route through `gen_result_let_binding`, so `let r: Result<...> = (Ok(...) as Result<...>);` lowers to `r, err := Result[...] { ... }` and fails Go build with an assignment-mismatch error.

Annotated/ascribed direct `Result` match scrutinees still take the old multi-return branch in `gen_result_match`, so `match (Ok(42) as Result<i64, str>) { ... }` lowers to `if val, err := Result[...] { ... }; err == nil`, which produces invalid Go syntax.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The added regressions cover naked `Ok`/`Err` constructor forms and variable matches, but they still miss the annotated/ascribed direct let-binding and direct match-scrutinee shapes from the original failure.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the "Write tests first" procedure so workers either add a minimal failing test before patching or explicitly record a deviation when they need to investigate and patch codegen first.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `misc-direct-result-value-position-lowering` review observed that session `26ef7288-20d3-4c3a-8eff-01f37f51a67e` edited `lib/codegen.ml` repeatedly before later adding regressions, while the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` despite `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md` step 3 saying to write tests first.
